info.name: Task Tracker API
info.description:
Backend API for the Task Tracker assessment project: auth/session, RBAC administration, and task CRUD with real-time WebSocket events.

**Run order matters** (this collection chains ids/tokens between requests via collection variables, visible under the collection's Variables tab):
1. Auth > Register, then Auth > Login — creates a regular user, captures its access token (`accessToken`) and id (`userId`).
2. Admin > Login as Admin — requires `adminEmail`/`adminPassword` set in the active environment (your ADMIN_SEED_EMAIL/ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD from apps/backend/.env). Overwrites `accessToken` with the admin's.
3. RBAC Administration and Users folders — run as the admin (step 2), targeting the regular user captured in step 1 (`{{userId}}`). Run them top-to-bottom within each folder; several later requests depend on ids captured by earlier ones in the same folder.
4. Tasks folder — re-run Auth > Login (regular user, step 1) first if you want to exercise the `:own`-scoped paths instead of the admin's `:any` scope.

Every folder below explicitly declares Bearer Token auth (visible on the folder's own Authorization tab) sourced from `{{accessToken}}` — Health and the public Auth endpoints (Register/Login/Refresh) override it to No Auth at the request level, everything else inherits it. The refresh token is a cookie (`refresh_token`), handled by Postman's own cookie jar; nothing to configure for that.

**Access tokens expire after 15 minutes.** If a request that worked earlier suddenly returns 401 partway through a manual run, that's why — rerun Auth > Login (or Admin > Login as Admin) to get a fresh token, not a bug in the request itself.

item.name: Health
item.description: Ops plumbing, not part of the versioned /api/v1 surface. No auth required.

request.description: Container/orchestrator healthcheck.

item.name: Auth
item.description: Registration, login/logout, and session refresh. Register/Login/Refresh are public (No Auth, set explicitly on each of those three requests); Get Me/Logout/Logout All require the Bearer token this folder declares.

request.description: Creates a new user, always assigned the USER role regardless of any `role` field sent. Does not log in — call Login next. 409 on a duplicate email. `testEmail` is regenerated fresh (timestamp-based) by this request's pre-request script every time it runs, so re-running never collides.

Requires Register to have run first in this session (uses the same `{{testEmail}}`). Issues a 15-minute access token (JSON body) and a refresh token (httpOnly cookie, never in the body). Captures accessToken and userId into collection variables on success — every other folder in this collection depends on this having run.

Reads the refresh_token cookie only (set by Login). Rotates it and returns a new access token. A reused/expired token revokes every session for that user and returns 401 — use this if your accessToken went stale (15 min) instead of Login again, to avoid creating a new user.
Returns the caller's user record, role names, and fully-resolved effective permissions — what the frontend uses to drive all permission-gated UI.

Revokes only the current session (the refresh token behind the request's cookie). Run Login again afterward if you want to keep using this collection — accessToken is not cleared client-side, but the session behind it is now dead server-side.
Revokes every session for the caller and force-disconnects their live WebSocket connections. Same caveat as Logout — run Login again afterward.

item.name: Admin
item.description: One-off login as the seeded admin account. Run this after Auth > Register/Login, before RBAC Administration or Users.

request.description: Logs in as the seeded admin account. `adminEmail`/`adminPassword` must be set in the active Postman Environment (not the collection) to your ADMIN_SEED_EMAIL/ADMIN_SEED_PASSWORD — check the environment selector dropdown top-right in Postman is set to 'Task Tracker - Local' (or your renamed copy of it) before running this, otherwise those two variables resolve to empty strings and this call 401s. Overwrites the shared accessToken with the admin's.

item.name: RBAC Administration
item.description: Role and role-permission management. Every request here requires role:manage and inherits Bearer auth from {{accessToken}} — run Admin > Login as Admin first. Run top-to-bottom: Create Role populates {{roleId}}, which Rename/Replace Permissions/Assign/Delete all depend on.

request.description: Requires role:manage OR permission:assign. Returns the fixed, code-defined permission catalog.
Requires role:manage. Returns every role with its assigned permission keys.

Requires role:manage. Creates a new role, isSystem: false, no permissions assigned yet. `roleName` is regenerated fresh (timestamp-based) by this request's pre-request script every time it runs, so a role left behind by an interrupted previous run (e.g. Delete Role never reached) can't collide with this one. Captures the new role's id as {{roleId}} — every other request in this folder depends on this having run.
Requires role:manage. Omitting name, or sending its current value, is a deliberate no-op (200 with the unchanged role, not an error).
Requires role:manage. Full replacement of the role's permission set — invalidates the Redis permission cache for every user currently holding this role.

Requires role:manage (deliberately, not user:manage). Full replacement of the target user's role assignments — invalidates that user's permission cache immediately. Uses {{userId}}, captured by Auth > Login, as the target.

Requires role:manage. 403 if the role is a system role (USER/ADMIN). Otherwise deletes it and cascades its UserRole assignments — run this last, after Assign User Roles, or that assignment will dangle on a deleted role.

item.name: Users
item.description: User administration and per-user permission overrides. Every request here requires an admin-scope permission (user:manage or permission:assign) and inherits Bearer auth from {{accessToken}} — run Admin > Login as Admin first. Run top-to-bottom: Upsert Permission Override must run before Get User Detail, which captures {{permissionOverrideId}} for Delete Permission Override.

request.description: Requires user:manage. Paginated user list with each user's assigned role names.

Requires task:read:any OR user:manage. Exactly one of `ids` (comma-separated) or `q` (free-text, capped at 10 results) must be present — 400 if neither or both are. Used by the frontend to resolve a task's ownerId to a display name.

Requires permission:assign. Upserts a direct per-user override; DENY always wins over any role-derived grant for the same key. Invalidates that user's permission cache immediately. This returns 204 with no body — run Get User Detail next to fetch the resulting override's id.

Requires user:manage. Returns the user's roles and direct permission overrides. Must run after Upsert Permission Override (not before) — it captures the first override's id into {{permissionOverrideId}} for the Delete Permission Override request below.

Requires permission:assign. Removes a direct override, reverting that user to role-derived permissions only for that key. Depends on {{permissionOverrideId}} from Get User Detail — a blank id here (404) means Get User Detail didn't run, or ran before Upsert Permission Override.
Requires user:manage. Admin-triggered equivalent of Auth > Logout All, targeting a different user's sessions.

item.name: Tasks
item.description: Task CRUD. Requires task:create/task:read:*/task:update:*/task:delete:* depending on the request, and inherits Bearer auth from {{accessToken}} — works with either the regular user (Auth > Login, :own scope) or the admin (Admin > Login as Admin, :any scope). Run Create Task first to populate {{taskId}}.

request.description: Requires task:create. Owner defaults to the caller; an explicit `ownerId` in the body is only honored for a caller with task:read:any. status defaults to TODO. Past dueDate values are allowed. Emits a task.created WebSocket event. Captures the new task's id as {{taskId}} — every other request in this folder depends on this having run.

exec: // 200 if the reviewer has set GEMINI_API_KEY on the backend; 503 (not a failure of
// this collection) if that optional env var is unset. Either response means the endpoint
pm.test('response has polished title and description', () => {

request.description: Requires task:create OR task:update:own OR task:update:any (same as create/update, no dedicated permission key). Stateless — never reads or writes a Task row, no WebSocket event. Optional bonus feature: returns 503 rather than an error if GEMINI_API_KEY is not configured server-side. Never persists the result; resubmit the polished title/description via Create Task or Update Task to actually save it.
Requires task:read:own OR task:read:any. A caller with only :own gets 404 (not 403) for a task they don't own — indistinguishable from a task that doesn't exist at all.

Requires task:read:own OR task:read:any. A caller with only :own is always forced to their own ownerId regardless of the ownerId query param. totalPages is at least 1 even when total is 0. Default sort createdAt DESC. Enable the disabled `status`/`ownerId` query params above to try filtering.

Requires task:update:own OR task:update:any, same ownership gate as Get Task. Partial update — any subset of title/description/status/dueDate/ownerId. ownerId itself only editable with task:update:any. Emits a task.updated WebSocket event. `version` is required (optimistic concurrency) — it must match the task's current version exactly, or this returns 409. Hardcoded to 1 here since Create Task always runs immediately before this in the suggested run order; if you've already run Update Task once against this task, bump it to 2, and so on.

Requires task:delete:own OR task:delete:any, same ownership gate. Soft delete (deletedAt set, never returned in any response) — the task then 404s from every read/write endpoint. Emits a task.deleted WebSocket event. Run this last in the folder — every other Tasks request above depends on {{taskId}} still existing.
